_meta.disclaimer: Indicative 2026 costs based on university accommodation pages and typical student rents. Verify current rates before committing.

cities.name: Auckland
hallPros: Easy to make friends in first year
hallCons: Most expensive of any NZ city
flattingCons: Cooking + bills on top of rent
Rental market in Auckland is very tight
cities.verdict: In Auckland, hall first year is worth it if you can afford it — the social start matters. Flat from year 2 to save money.

cities.name: Wellington
hallPros: Walking distance to Kelburn and CBD campuses
hallCons: Wellington flats can be cold — but halls are heated
flattingPros: Lots of student flats in Aro Valley and Mount Victoria
flattingCons: Wellington flats are notoriously cold and draughty — check insulation
cities.verdict: Wellington has excellent hall options for first year and a strong flatting culture for years 2+. Inspect flats for insulation before signing.

cities.name: Christchurch
hallPros: UC halls are well-run and include meals
Ilam campus is leafy and close to halls
hallCons: More limited night-life compared to Auckland/Wellington
flattingPros: Flatting is affordable by NZ standards
Ilam + Riccarton student areas are walkable to campus
flattingCons: Post-quake rental stock is newer but varies
cities.verdict: Christchurch strikes the best balance of affordable flatting and strong halls. Either choice works financially.

cities.name: Dunedin
hallPros: Otago halls are iconic and social
Walking distance to all campus + town
flattingCons: Many older flats are poorly insulated — cold winters
Inspect for heating and damp before signing
cities.verdict: Dunedin is the most affordable student city in NZ. Hall first year, flat thereafter is the classic Otago path.

cities.name: Hamilton
hallPros: Small close-knit hall community at Waikato
cities.verdict: Hamilton is a quiet, affordable option — great if you want to focus on study and save money.

cities.name: Palmerston North
hallPros: Massey halls are spread over spacious campus
hallCons: City is smaller and quieter — weekends can feel empty
flattingPros: Rent is among the cheapest in NZ
Student flatting is established around campus
flattingCons: Winters are cold and flats can be damp
cities.verdict: Palmerston North is a low-cost option with well-organised halls. Flatting is excellent value.

cities.name: Lincoln
hallPros: Small, rural-style campus with tight-knit halls
hallCons: Very small town — limited off-campus life
flattingPros: Nearby Christchurch for shopping + social life
flattingCons: You may need a car to live further out
cities.verdict: Lincoln is a specialist university — hall is usually best for the rural + ag-science community feel.
